tool_input.summary: PPI holds Juno electron-density products derived from Juno/Waves characteristic plasma frequencies (PDS4) plus supporting calibrated JADE plasma moments and FGM magnetic-field data (PDS3). These are the most direct archives to quantify auroral-zone electron-density depletions and compare inside/outside the auroral acceleration region.

candidates.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Jupiter Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Primary curated electron density (ne) time series derived from Juno/Waves plasma-wave cutoffs/resonances (e.g., fpe, fuh, flhr) for Jovicentric (Jupiter) observations—best starting point to identify and measure density cavities/depletions on polar/auroral passes.

candidates.title: JUNO JADE DERIVED MOMENTS SCIENCE DATA
candidates.reasoning: Provides JADE-derived plasma moments (including number density products where applicable) to compare with Waves-derived ne and to relate depletions to particle populations/auroral acceleration signatures.

candidates.title: JUNO MAGNETOMETER CALIBRATED DATA
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated magnetic field needed to compute electron cyclotron frequency fce and to map observations to auroral field lines; also directly referenced as essential for density derivations in the Waves electron-density bundle.

candidates.title: JUNO WAVES SURVEY STANDARD SCIENCE PRODUCTS
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated/derived Waves survey products (spectrograms etc.) useful for independently confirming when characteristic frequencies disappear/shift during auroral density cavities and for context around density-depletion intervals.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Planetary Plasma Interactions (PPI) node at https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/

Directory layout and workflow:
All ~781 datasets sit directly under data/ with no mission sub-directories.
The mission 'name' from pds_list_missions is the filter keyword to use with list_dataset_dirs.


Dataset naming conventions:
  PDS3 dirs use uppercase mission codes: MESS-, CO-, GO-, JNO-, VG1-, VG2-, MEX-, P10-, P11-, ULY-, NH-, PVO-, etc.
  PDS4 dirs use lowercase names: cassini-, galileo-, juno-, maven-, messenger-, ulysses-, etc.
  Both conventions exist for many missions. Use pds_list_missions to see all available missions and filter keywords.


PDS3 vs PDS4:
  - PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style hyphenated identifiers (e.g. mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-dtm-v1). Leaf marker: voldesc.cat or voldesc.sfd.
  - PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`. Leaf marker: bundle*.xml or bundle*.lblx. Collections live in subdirectories.
  - Hybrid: some directories have BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 labels.

YOUR TOOLS (4 tools — always pass node='ppi'):
  1. pds_list_missions(node='ppi') — returns mission directories with descriptions. No HTTP.
  2. pds_list_dataset_dirs(path, node='ppi', filter=...) — lists sub-directory names under a path. Cheap HTTP, no label parsing.
  3. pds_probe_datasets(paths, node='ppi') — probes specific directories for PDS labels. Accepts a list of paths (max 20).
  4. pds_inspect_collections(path, node='ppi') — scans PDS4 bundle subdirs for collection labels.

WORKFLOW:
  Step 1: Call pds_list_missions(node='ppi') to see all available missions and their filter keywords.
  Step 2: Identify which mission(s) are relevant to the query.
  Step 3: Call pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<mission_name>') using the mission name as filter keyword.
  Step 4: Call pds_probe_datasets with the most relevant paths (batch up to 20).
  Step 5: If PDS4 bundles found, call pds_inspect_collections on top 2-3.
  Step 6: Return candidates.

CRITICAL RULES:
  1. EVERY query requires at least one candidate. Never dismiss a query.
  2. Prefer CALIBRATED/REDUCED data over raw/EDR for science queries.
  3. For PDS4, use inspect_collections ONLY for top 2-3 bundles. Return COLLECTION-level LID when :data or :calibrated exists.
  4. When both PDS3 and PDS4 versions exist, prefer the PDS4 collection-level identifier.
  5. Stay under 8 tool calls per query.
  6. Only return paths you've actually probed — never guess.
Do Juno observations show electron density depletions over Jupiter's auroral zones, and if so how large are they?

missions.name: cassini
missions.description: Cassini at Saturn (CAPS, MAG, MIMI-CHEMS/INCA/LEMMS, RPWS, INMS). Also filter 'CO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: galileo
missions.description: Galileo at Jupiter + flybys of Earth/Venus/asteroids (EPD, MAG, PLS, PWS, PPR, HIC, SSD, RSS). Also filter 'GO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: juno
missions.description: Juno at Jupiter (Waves, JADE/JAD, JEDI/JED, FGM, ASC). Also filter 'JNO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: VG1
missions.description: Voyager 1 at Jupiter, Saturn, and interplanetary (CRS, LECP, MAG, PLS, PWS, PRA, RSS). Also filter 'vg1' for PDS4.

missions.name: VG2
missions.description: Voyager 2 at Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, and interplanetary (CRS, LECP, MAG, PLS, PWS, PRA, RSS). Also filter 'vg2' for PDS4.

missions.name: MESS
missions.description: MESSENGER at Mercury (EPPS incl. FIPS & EPS, MAG). Also filter 'messenger' for PDS4 bundle. Target: Mercury.

missions.name: MEX
missions.description: Mars Express (ASPERA-3 incl. ELS/IMA/NPI, MARSIS). Target: Mars.

missions.name: maven
missions.description: MAVEN at Mars (MAG, LPW, SEP, STATIC, SWEA, SWIA, EUV, ROSE). Target: Mars.

missions.name: P10
missions.description: Pioneer 10 at Jupiter (CPI, CRT, GTT, HVM, PA, TRD, UV). Also filter 'p10' for PDS4.

missions.name: P11
missions.description: Pioneer 11 at Jupiter and Saturn (CPI, CRT, FGM, GTT, HVM, PA, TRD, UV). Also filter 'p11' for PDS4.

missions.name: ULY
missions.description: Ulysses at Jupiter and interplanetary (COSPIN, EPAC, HISCALE, SWOOPS, VHM-FGM, URAP, GAS, GRB, SCE, SWICS). Also filter 'ulysses' for PDS4.

missions.name: NH
missions.description: New Horizons at Jupiter and Pluto (PEPSSI, SWAP). Target: Jupiter, Pluto.

missions.name: PVO
missions.description: Pioneer Venus Orbiter (OEFD, OETP, OIMS, OMAG, ONMS, ORPA, ORSE). Also filter 'pvo' for PDS4. Target: Venus.

missions.name: LP
missions.description: Lunar Prospector (MAG, ER — electron reflectometer). Also filter 'lp' for PDS4. Target: Moon.

missions.name: MGS
missions.description: Mars Global Surveyor (MAG/ER, RSS). Also filter 'mgs' for PDS4. Target: Mars.

missions.name: NEAR
missions.description: NEAR Shoemaker (MAG). Target: Eros, Earth flyby.

missions.name: M10
missions.description: Mariner 10 (MAG, PLS). Target: Mercury.

missions.name: LRO
missions.description: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter (CRaTER). Also filter 'lro' for PDS4. Target: Moon.

missions.name: ODY
missions.description: Mars Odyssey (MARIE — radiation). Target: Mars.

missions.name: MSL
missions.description: Mars Science Laboratory / Curiosity (RAD — radiation). Target: Mars.

missions.name: insight
missions.description: InSight (IFG — fluxgate magnetometer). Target: Mars.

missions.name: vex
missions.description: Venus Express (ASPERA-4 ELS, MAG). Target: Venus.

missions.name: ICE
missions.description: International Cometary Explorer (EPAS, MAG, PLAWAV, RADWAV, SWPLAS). Target: Giacobini-Zinner.

missions.name: GIO
missions.description: Giotto (IMS incl. HERS/HIS, JPA, MAG). Target: Halley.

missions.name: VEGA

collections.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Europa Browse Plots

Identification_Area.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Europa Browse Plots

Citation_Information.description:
This collection of browse products provides a visual overview of 
            plasma density values and spacecraft trajectories during flybys
            of Europa by Juno while in orbit about Jupiter.  Each image
            consists of two sub-plots.  One of which orients the Juno's 
            trajectory in plasma corotational coordinates, the other plots 
            density versus time and provides higher data value resolution.
            The geometric plasma wake is indicated in both plots.  This is 
            merely a cylinder starting at Europa and extending in the
            corotation direction, with the same radius as the moon itself.
            This is only intended to provide a rough guidline on when wake
            effects may be present.

            All plots were generated courtesy of the open-source Matplotlib
            Python package.

collections.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Browse Plots

Identification_Area.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Browse Plots

Citation_Information.description:
This collection of browse products provides a visual overview of the
            plasma density values in the associated data collection.  Due to rapid
            changes in relative velocity over the course of Juno's orbit, data are
            presented in three coordinate systems: Jovigraphic, magnetic and temporal.
            It is intended that at least one sub-plot from each image will sufficently
            spread data in coordiantes to acertain thier scientific utility at a glance.
            Failing this, the data themselves consist of simple delimited text files
            that should be directly readable by almost any scientific data processing
            system.  Since the absence of a measurement at a location is also useful
            knowledge, Juno's trajectory is provided on spatial plots.

            All plots were generated courtesy of the open-source matplotlib python
            package.

collections.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Europa Data Collection

Identification_Area.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Europa Data Collection

Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains electron plasma density data along with
        the key plasma frequencies used to derive these densities from Juno/Waves
        observations.  Data products in this collection are formatted as simple
        delimited text that should be readable by almost any scientific data analysis
        package.

        Within the files are the originally digitized data points along with the
        resulting characteristic frequencies from cold plasma theory including, but
        not limited to, the plasma electron frequency, the lower-hybrid resonance,
        and the upper-hybrid resonance.  Electron cyclotron values provided by the
        Juno FGM instrument team are essential for most calculations.  For a full
        description of the source, usefulness and limitations of data in this
        collection see the associated documents collection provided in this bundle.

        Measurements in this small collection were derived from data gathered
        during one or more flybys of Europa and thus are provided along with
        plasma corotational coordinates.  Since flybys occur infrequency, most
        of the data in the bundle may be found in the primary Jovicentric 
        collection.

        All data values were digitized manually using the open-source Autoplot
        program.

parts.args: {"summary":"PPI holds Juno electron-density products derived from Juno/Waves characteristic plasma frequencies (PDS4) plus supporting calibrated JADE plasma moments and FGM magnetic-field data (PDS3). These are the most direct archives to quantify auroral-zone electron-density depletions and compare inside/outside the auroral acceleration region.","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:juno_waves_electron-density:data_jupiter","path":"data/juno-waves-electron-density/","title":"Juno Waves Electron Densities, Jupiter Data Collection","mission":"juno","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Primary curated electron density (ne) time series derived from Juno/Waves plasma-wave cutoffs/resonances (e.g., fpe, fuh, flhr) for Jovicentric (Jupiter) observations—best starting point to identify and measure density cavities/depletions on polar/auroral passes."},{"dataset_id":"JNO-J-JAD-5-MOMENTS-V1.0","path":"data/JNO-J-JAD-5-MOMENTS-V1.0/","title":"JUNO JADE DERIVED MOMENTS SCIENCE DATA","mission":"juno","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"Provides JADE-derived plasma moments (including number density products where applicable) to compare with Waves-derived ne and to relate depletions to particle populations/auroral acceleration signatures."},{"dataset_id":"JNO-J-3-FGM-CAL-V1.0","path":"data/JNO-J-3-FGM-CAL-V1.0/","title":"JUNO MAGNETOMETER CALIBRATED DATA","mission":"juno","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"Calibrated magnetic field needed to compute electron cyclotron frequency fce and to map observations to auroral field lines; also directly referenced as essential for density derivations in the Waves electron-density bundle."},{"dataset_id":"JNO-E/J/SS-WAV-3-CDR-SRVFULL-V2.0","path":"data/JNO-E_J_SS-WAV-3-CDR-SRVFULL-V2.0/","title":"JUNO WAVES SURVEY STANDARD SCIENCE PRODUCTS","mission":"juno","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"Calibrated/derived Waves survey products (spectrograms etc.) useful for independently confirming when characteristic frequencies disappear/shift during auroral density cavities and for context around density-depletion intervals."}]}
